responses: Hello! Welcome to the College Info Desk. How can I help you today?
Hi there! Ask me about admissions, courses, fees, timings, or facilities.
Goodbye! Feel free to come back if you have more questions.
Take care! Good luck with your college journey.
You're welcome!
Happy to help! Anything else you'd like to know?

patterns: how do I apply for admission
responses: Admissions open every year in June. You need to fill the online application form on the college website, upload your 12th marksheet, and appear for the entrance test/counseling as applicable.
To apply: visit the admissions portal, submit your academic documents, and pay the application fee. Merit lists are published within two weeks of the deadline.
We offer undergraduate programs in Computer Science, Information Technology, Electronics, Mechanical, Civil, and Business Administration, along with postgraduate programs in select departments.
Available courses include B.Tech (CSE, IT, ECE, Mech, Civil), BBA, and MBA. Ask me about a specific course for more details.
The annual tuition fee varies by course, ranging from 60,000 to 1,50,000 rupees. Detailed fee structure is available on the college website under 'Admissions > Fees'.
Fees can be paid online via the student portal in one installment or two semester-wise installments. Merit and need-based scholarships are also available.
College hours are 9:00 AM to 4:30 PM, Monday to Saturday. The administrative office is open from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM.
Classes run from 9 AM to 4:30 PM on weekdays, with a half day on Saturdays.
The library is open from 8:30 AM to 7:00 PM on all working days. Students can issue up to 3 books for 15 days using their student ID.
The central library has over 50,000 books, digital journals, a reading hall, and free Wi-Fi for students.
Yes, separate hostels are available for boys and girls with mess facilities, Wi-Fi, and 24/7 security. You can apply through the hostel section of the admission form.
Hostel fees range from 45,000 to 70,000 rupees per year depending on room type (shared/single).
Semester exams are usually held in December and May. The exact schedule is published on the notice board and student portal a month in advance.
Results are declared within 3-4 weeks of the last exam. Revaluation requests can be submitted online within 10 days of result declaration.
Our placement cell has tie-ups with over 100 companies including TCS, Infosys, Wipro, and various startups. The average package last year was 4.5 LPA with the highest at 18 LPA.
The Training & Placement Cell also arranges internships, resume-building workshops, and mock interviews for final-year students.
You can reach us at info@college.edu.in or call the admissions office at +91-9876543210. The campus is located at College Road, Pune, Maharashtra.
For any queries, email admissions@college.edu.in or visit the administrative office during working hours.
Our annual technical fest 'TechVerse' and cultural fest 'Rhythm' are held every year in February and September respectively. Sports meets are conducted every October.
Check the Events tab on the college website or the notice board for updates on upcoming workshops, seminars, and fests.
Each department has 15-20 qualified faculty members, most holding PhDs or M.Tech degrees. Department-wise faculty details are listed on the college website under 'Departments'.
You can find individual faculty contact details and office hours on the department page of the website.

patterns: what if my attendance is low
responses: As per university rules, a minimum of 75% attendance is mandatory to appear for the semester examinations.
You need to maintain 75% attendance in both lectures and practicals. Low attendance might result in being debarred from exams unless a valid medical certificate is provided.

patterns: where can I get food on campus
responses: Yes, the campus has a spacious central cafeteria serving hygienic veg and non-veg meals, snacks, and beverages from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
The college canteen and juice center are located near the sports ground. Both breakfast and lunch options are available at subsidized rates for students.
Our college follows a strict zero-tolerance policy towards ragging. The campus is 100% ragging-free, and any violation leads to immediate expulsion and legal action.
Ragging is a punishable offense. If you face any issues, you can immediately contact the Anti-Ragging Committee members or drop a complaint in the grievance box near the office.
Yes, separate free parking slots are available for two-wheelers and four-wheelers. Students must display their college vehicle pass to park inside the campus.
Ample parking space is provided near the main gate. Please ensure you wear a helmet (for two-wheelers) to enter the campus.
I am Campus Assistant, your virtual info desk helper! I can guide you through admissions, fees, placements, document uploads, and daily campus rules.
Think of me as your digital guide to the college. I handle quick questions about campus life, and you can even upload administrative files through me!

patterns: what documents are required for admission
which certificates should I upload for admission
responses: For admission verification, you need to upload: 1) 10th & 12th Marksheets, 2) School Leaving/Transfer Certificate, 3) Migration Certificate (if applicable), 4) Passport size photographs, and 5) Caste/Category Certificate (if claiming quota). You can use the paperclip icon here to upload them.

The mandatory admission documents are your 10th and 12th grade passing certificates, Leaving Certificate, and a valid ID proof (Aadhar Card/Passport). Please scan and upload them through this chat window.

patterns: what to bring to the exam hall
do I need an exam pass
responses: To enter the examination hall, you must carry your printed Hall Ticket (Admit Card) and your official College Student ID Card. No digital copies on phones are allowed.
The mandatory documents for appearing in semester exams are your physical Hall Ticket and College ID Card. Ensure your hall ticket has the Controller of Examinations' signature/stamp.

patterns: how to apply for scholarship documents
what papers do I need for financial aid
responses: For scholarship applications, you must submit: 1) Family Income Certificate (issued by a competent authority), 2) Previous year's Marksheet (minimum 60-75% required depending on the scheme), 3) Domicile Certificate, 4) Aadhar Card linked to your bank account, and 5) Fee Receipt of the current year.

Scholarship approval requires your Income Certificate, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Domicile Certificate, and Cap Round/Admission Letter. Upload them here so the administration cell can review your eligibility.

patterns: what certificates are required for hostel room allocation
responses: For hostel allotment, you need to submit: 1) Copy of the College Admission Fee Receipt, 2) Residential Address Proof (Aadhar/Electricity Bill), 3) Medical Fitness Certificate signed by a registered practitioner, and 4) Two local guardian passport photos.
To secure your hostel room, please provide the college admission confirmation letter, a medical clearance certificate, and an address proof document.
